summary: Extended native ordinary-program coverage for the feedback loop/process demo and hardened the native runtime’s process launcher so repo-relative executables still resolve when a Clasp program runs tools from a different child `cwd`. Added a runtime regression test for that path and updated `scripts/test-native-claspc.sh` to exercise compiled-native feedback-loop and process-demo scenarios with absolute runtime paths.
residual_risks: I could not run the new Rust unit test directly because `cargo` is not available in this environment.

I did not complete a full `bash scripts/test-native-claspc.sh` run in-turn; the script now contains the native-loop assertions, but end-to-end execution remains to be re-run in CI or a fuller local environment.
`scripts/test-native-claspc.sh` already had unrelated in-flight edits in this workspace; I preserved them and layered the new coverage on top.

feedback.summary: This attempt moved verification closer to closing `ordinary_program_execution`/`verification_gate` by covering the compiled-native loop path and fixing a native process-launch path-resolution mismatch.
ergonomics: Native process execution now tolerates repo-relative tool paths even when the launched Clasp program changes child working directories.
The feedback-loop native scenario now uses absolute runtime fixture paths so the control-plane test behaves consistently across backends.
follow_ups: Run the full native/runtime verification path, especially `bash scripts/test-native-claspc.sh`, in an environment with the normal toolchain available.
Run the runtime Rust test suite once `cargo` is available to confirm the new `spawn_command_json_resolves_relative_programs_from_launcher_cwd` regression test passes.
warnings: The repository-wide `verify-all` gate was not run because the task explicitly said not to run it.
